claims.text: The artifact must declare that calibration evidence is missing when calibration is not present.
A missing support record cannot be promoted into a supported claim.
Declared losses should constrain the recommended action rather than being appended as decorative text.

parsed.conclusion: The artifact is constrained to its three supporting evidence records. Declared losses are non-decorative: they actively limit the scope, confidence, and actionability of every claim. No missing support record has been promoted into a claim, and no assertion extends beyond the available corpus. The absence of calibration evidence means no external validation is claimed; the absence of prior cycle state means the artifact carries no inherited warrants.
parsed.recommended_action: Limit all artifact claims to the three evidence records present in the corpus (201, 202, 203). Do not extrapolate beyond them. Treat calibration_evidence_missing as a standing constraint that caps confidence and prevents any claim of external validation. Because no_prior_cycle_state is declared, the artifact is built de novo and carries no inherited warrants — any future cycle that builds on it must re-verify these claims independently. Do not cite the distractor record (e8100000-0000-0000-0000-000000000299).

content.text: The artifact must declare that calibration evidence is missing when calibration is not present.
A missing support record cannot be promoted into a supported claim.
Declared losses should constrain the recommended action rather than being appended as decorative text.

request_payload.instruction: Write the final artifact. Cite only evidence records that support claims. Do not cite distractors. If a limitation remains, include it in declared_losses.
